2603.22623.title: To Agree or To Be Right? The Grounding-Sycophancy Tradeoff in Medical Vision-Language Models
2603.22623.paper_summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) adapted to the medical domain have shown strong performance on visual question answering benchmarks, yet their robustness against two critical failure modes, hallucination and sycophancy, remains poorly understood, particularly in combination. We evaluate six VLMs (three general-purpose, three medical-specialist) on three medical VQA datasets and uncover a grounding-sycophancy tradeoff: models with the lowest hallucination propensity are the most sycophantic, while the most pressure-resistant model hallucinates more than all medical-specialist models. To characterize this tradeoff, we propose three metrics: L-VASE, a logit-space reformulation of VASE that avoids its double-normalization; CCS, a confidence-calibrated sycophancy score that penalizes high-confidence capitulation; and Clinical Safety Index (CSI), a unified safety index that combines grounding, autonomy, and calibration via a geometric mean. Across 1,151 test cases, no model achieves a CSI above 0.35, indicating that none of the evaluated 7-8B parameter VLMs is simultaneously well-grounded and robust to social pressure. Our findings suggest that joint evaluation of both properties is necessary before these models can be considered for clinical use. Code is available at https://github.com/UTSA-VIRLab/AgreeOrRight
2603.22623.paper_summary_zh: 視覺語言模型（VLMs）在醫療領域的適應性已在視覺問題回答基準上顯示出強大的表現，但它們對於兩種關鍵失效模式——幻覺和拍馬屁的穩健性仍然不甚了解，特別是它們的結合。我們在三個醫療VQA數據集上評估了六個VLM（其中三個為通用型，三個為醫療專家型），並揭示了一個基礎-拍馬屁的權衡：具有最低幻覺傾向的模型是最拍馬屁的，而最抗壓的模型則比所有醫療專家模型更容易產生幻覺。為了描述這個權衡，我們提出了三個指標：L-VASE，一種避免雙重正規化的VASE的對數空間重構；CCS，一種罰款高置信度屈服的信心校準拍馬屁分數；以及臨床安全指數（CSI），一個通用的安全指數，通過幾何平均結合基礎、自治和校準。在1,151個測試案例中，沒有任何模型的CSI超過0.35，這表明所評估的7-8B參數VLM中沒有一個同時具備良好的基礎和對社會壓力的穩健性。我們的發現表明，在這些模型可以考慮用於臨床使用之前，對這兩種特性進行聯合評估是必要的。代碼可在 https://github.com/UTSA-VIRLab/AgreeOrRight 獲得。
2603.22623.author: OFM Riaz Rahman Aranya et.al.
2603.22623.authors: OFM Riaz Rahman Aranya, Kevin Desai
